rotation_risk.name: Haaland
rotation_risk.reason: Man City confirmed heavy rotation for GW38 with Guardiola making sweeping changes. Haaland was not included in the matchday squad — a high-ownership implosion for FPL captains who held him. Man City had nothing left to play for in the league.

rotation_risk.name: Gabriel
rotation_risk.reason: Arsenal are Premier League champions and face PSG in the UCL final. Arteta rotated widely, resting Saliba, Saka and Raya. Gabriel's features data already flags him as 'doubtful' (75% chance, status d) with -140k net transfers — double risk flag. High probability of bench or absence.

rotation_risk.name: Mateta
rotation_risk.reason: Crystal Palace face the UEFA Conference League final the following Wednesday. Glasner acknowledged rest considerations. Mateta is central to their system and no injury concern (status a, 100%), but early substitution or precautionary management is plausible.

rotation_risk.name: Rogers
rotation_risk.reason: Aston Villa's final-day motivation depends on their league table position. Rogers has the fewest starts (29) and minutes (2600) in the watchlist. End-of-season squad rotation is possible. Favourable home fixture (FDR 2) is the only upside signal. Monitor late team news.

confirmed_starters.name: M.Salah
confirmed_starters.note: Started in what was widely reported as his Liverpool farewell game. Slot initially declined to confirm participation but Salah was named in the XI. Penalty taker (pens_order 1) and set-piece threat. Most assured starter in the watchlist at 36 starts, 3200 minutes, 232 pts.

confirmed_starters.name: Mbeumo
confirmed_starters.note: Brentford have no European obligations or cup distractions. Mbeumo is their primary creative force — status available (100%), primary set-piece taker (set_piece_note 1) and penalty taker (pens_order 1). Home fixture FDR 2. Nailed budget enabler at 7.8m.

key_absences.name: Haaland
key_absences.reason: Omitted from Man City's matchday squad for GW38. Guardiola rotation — City had no remaining league objectives. Catastrophic for high-ownership FPL captains.

key_absences.name: Gabriel
key_absences.reason: Doubtful tag in features (75% chance, status d) plus Arsenal-wide UCL final rotation policy. Arteta rested defensive starters including Saliba. Gabriel's involvement highly uncertain — net -140k transfers confirm widespread FPL manager exits.

key_absences.name: Saka
key_absences.reason: Not in the watchlist but flagged for context: Saka was rested by Arteta ahead of the UCL final. Heavily owned across FPL — signals the full scale of Arsenal's GW38 rotation policy.

notes: CONTEXT: Arsenal won the 2025-26 Premier League title and face PSG in the UCL final in Budapest. Arteta confirmed widespread rotation — Saliba, Saka, Raya, and Gabriel all at risk of benching.
CONTEXT: Man City rotated heavily with Haaland omitted from the matchday squad entirely. Guardiola's final-game rotation is confirmed. Do not captain Haaland in GW38.
SALAH CONFIRMED: Despite pre-deadline uncertainty, Mohamed Salah started his likely farewell Liverpool appearance. Reliable captain option in GW38.
MBEUMO NAILED: Brentford's only reliable premium-mid option with no rotation risk, primary set-piece and pen duties, home FDR 2. Best budget enabler in the watchlist.
CRYSTAL PALACE: Conference League final the following week. Glasner may manage minutes for key players including Mateta. Watch for team news in the 24 hours before kickoff.

GW38 PATTERN: Final-day rotation is historically the highest unpredictability event in the FPL calendar. Only hold assets from clubs with genuine objectives (survival, European places) or no upcoming cup finals.
